head.text: Weight on vertex; aching and burning in temples and above eyes. Crushing feeling in vertex. Vertigo, dizzy when lying down

eyes.text: Ciliary neuralgia; worse any attempt to use eyes: burning in eyes. Lachrymation. Cannot bear light. Headache; involving nerves going to eye

female.text: Pain in milk ducts; intolerable between nursing. Pain in nipples

chest_heart.text: Sticking pain through right breast near sternum, extending to back near shoulders. Dyspnœa, and continuous cough, early in morning. Cough, with profuse and fetid expectoration; compels him to sit up. Hoarseness

fever_chill.text: Hectic; profuse and debilitating perspiration; intermittent, with pain in arms. Desire for acids

dosing.notes: Tincture, to sixth potency. In phthisis not below the sixth

keynotes.text: color="#800000">PHELLANDRIUM AQUATICUM Water Dropwort (PHELLANDRIUM) The respiratory symptoms are most important, and have been frequently verified clinically. A very good remedy for the offensive expectoration and cough in phthisis, bronchitis, and emphysema. Tuberculosis, affecting generally the middle lobes. Everything tastes sweet. Hæmoptysis, hectic and colliquative diarrhœa.

Text Book of Materia Medica. By Adolph Lippe, M. D. Professor of Materia Medica at the Homopathic College of Pennsylvania. Presented by Médi-T. Phellandrium aquaticum. Intolerable pains in the lactiferous tubes ; much worse during the act of nursing. Copyright © Médi-T 2006
